text.title: Sentry Supabase Vercel Wiring Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules for wiring Sentry into a Next.js-on-Vercel + Supabase stack so first incidents are useful, not noisy.

rule.statement: The repo MUST contain three Sentry configs: `sentry.client.config.ts`, `sentry.server.config.ts`, `sentry.edge.config.ts`. Single-config setups miss edge-runtime errors (middleware, edge API routes). Missing any file = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Next.js runs three different runtimes; Sentry's bundler-plugin emits separate transports. A single config silently drops edge-runtime errors — exactly where middleware-level auth fails.

rule.statement: Source maps MUST upload via Sentry's Next.js bundler plugin during the Vercel build (NOT a post-deploy hook). `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN`, `SENTRY_ORG`, `SENTRY_PROJECT` MUST live in Vercel project env (NOT in committed code). Builds without source-maps upload = REJECT — stack traces are minified garbage.
rule.rationale: Post-deploy hooks race the first user error. Vercel build is the only place that has both the unminified bundle and the deploy ID; everything else is hope.

rule.statement: `beforeSend` MUST scrub Supabase auth error payloads: strip `access_token`, `refresh_token`, `provider_token`, full email body, any `Authorization:` header echo. Unscrubbed Supabase errors leak JWTs into Sentry, which is a credential leak.
rule.rationale: Supabase client errors include the request that failed, which often contains the JWT. Sentry then becomes the leak. Scrubbing is mandatory, not optional.

rule.statement: Supabase `PGRST*` and `42501` (RLS denial) errors MUST be classified before sending to Sentry: legitimate RLS denials are expected behaviour (NOT errors), unexpected RLS denials indicate a real bug. The rule MUST tag `level: warning` for expected, `level: error` for unexpected, using a `meta.expected_rls` payload.
rule.rationale: Treating every RLS denial as an error floods Sentry with user-typo noise and hides the actual bugs. Classification is what makes Supabase + Sentry usable together.

rule.statement: `sentry.edge.config.ts` MUST set `tracesSampleRate` ≤ 0.1 (10%) regardless of client/server settings. Edge runtime executes per-request including middleware; 1.0 sampling on a moderate-traffic site exhausts quota in hours.
rule.rationale: Solo plans on Sentry have small spans quotas. Edge middleware runs on every request including bots — leaving sampling at 1.0 burns through monthly quota by lunchtime.

rule.statement: `ignoreErrors` MUST include the baseline noise list: ResizeObserver loop limit, AbortError on navigation, NextRouter cancel, hydration mismatch from extensions. New projects MUST start with this baseline; teams MAY only ADD, never remove without a rationale comment.
rule.rationale: Solo builders fight Sentry signal-to-noise for weeks before discovering the baseline list; codifying it saves the discovery cost and stops "Sentry is useless" abandonment.

example.title: Config skeleton (compressed)
